value_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: VLCC spot rates above $107,000/day vs $74,200 Q4 2025; 92% Q1 coverage locked; Cape of Good Hope rerouting extends voyage ton-miles; fleet renewal with scrubber-fitted newbuilds maximizes capture (Tier 4: Motley Fool, 24/7 Wall St.)

Discovery: Strait of Hormuz largely blocked since March 4 (US-Iran war) has forced global oil rerouting, pushing VLCC charter rates to records — Frontline, the world's largest listed supertanker owner, is up ~62% YTD but the conflict shows no near-term resolution, and longer routing permanently increases ton-miles per voyage, structurally supporting rates above pre-crisis levels.
value_picks.analyst_take: VLCC rates >$107K/day; Cape rerouting extends ton-miles structurally; Fwd P/E 11.9 with ~$5/share dividend capacity

Discovery: Director Sarah Farrell (via Waygrove Partnership LP) bought 50,500 shares across two days (Feb 10-11, 2026) at $139–$150/share for a total of $7.48M — the largest insider purchase of Reddit since its March 2024 IPO — while the stock was down ~46% from highs, with advertising growth and a Google content licensing partnership as active catalysts.
value_picks.analyst_take: Q1 revenue +69% YoY, massive beat on every metric; Google licensing + ad monetization compounding; director bought $7.48M

catalysts_general: Q1 record $1.282B revenue (+87% YoY), EPS $2.56 vs $2.11 est.; AI chip testing now >70% of revenue; stock fell 13-19% on Q2 sequential step-down guide; JP Morgan upgraded Overweight $400 PT April 30 (Tier 1: Q1 earnings transcript; Tier 3: JP Morgan)

value_picks.analyst_take: Post-earnings selloff on record quarter is overreaction; JP Morgan Overweight $400 PT; AI chip testing >70% of revenue

catalysts_general: Pattern Day Trader rule elimination effective June 4 — structural positive for active trader engagement and payment-for-order-flow revenue; stock down 52% from high; analyst consensus target $97.50 (Tier 4: financial news coverage)

Discovery: Robinhood sits 52% below its 52-week high ($153.86 → $73.37) after Q1 missed on crypto revenue (-47% YoY to $134M) — a cyclical miss, not structural decay; the SEC's elimination of the Pattern Day Trader rule (effective June 4, 2026) removes the $25,000 minimum equity requirement that had restricted millions from active trading since 2001, expanding Robinhood's core addressable market with zero Q2 impact yet in the numbers; the Trump Accounts program (60M eligible children, 5.5M enrolled, government cost-plus contract) adds structural recurring revenue; analyst median price target $131-$155 (+79-111%). Bear case: crypto stays depressed into Q2 masking core improvements; Trump Accounts execution slower than modeled.
value_picks.analyst_take: PDT rule elimination effective June 4 structural tailwind not yet in numbers; down 52%; analyst target $97.50
